ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., smartphone, laptop, tablet) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "battery_level" INTEGER /* Battery level of the device */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the device */,
  "last_seen_timestamp" INTEGER /* Last time the device was seen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_points" (
  "access_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access point */,
  "bssid" TEXT /* BSSID of the access point */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the access point */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of access point (e.g., router, switch, hub) */,
  "configuration" TEXT /* Configuration of the access point */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the access point */,
  "last_seen_timestamp" INTEGER /* Last time the access point was seen */,
  "channel" INTEGER /* Channel on which the access point operates */,
  "transmit_power" INTEGER /* Transmit power of the access point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device making the measurement */,
  "access_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point being measured */,
  "rssi" INTEGER /* RSSI value of the measurement */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance between the device and access point */,
  "x_coordinate" REAL /* X-coordinate of the device */,
  "y_coordinate" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the device */,
  "z_coordinate" REAL /* Z-coordinate of the device */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the measurement */,
  "ranging_timestamp_millis" INTEGER /* Ranging timestamp in milliseconds */,
  "measurement_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the measurement */,
  "measurement_variance" REAL /* Variance of the measurement */,
  "measurement_reliability" REAL /* Reliability score of the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_access_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_point_id") REFERENCES access_points ("access_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device at this location */,
  "x_coordinate" REAL /* X-coordinate of the device */,
  "y_coordinate" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the device */,
  "z_coordinate" REAL /* Z-coordinate of the device */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the location data */,
  "floor" INTEGER /* Floor number where the device is located */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* ID of the building where the device is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_locations_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_locations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_point_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "access_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access point at this location */,
  "x_coordinate" REAL /* X-coordinate of the access point */,
  "y_coordinate" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the access point */,
  "z_coordinate" REAL /* Z-coordinate of the access point */,
  "floor" INTEGER /* Floor number where the access point is located */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* ID of the building where the access point is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_point_locations_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_point_locations_access_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_point_id") REFERENCES access_points ("access_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_orientations" (
  "orientation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each orientation */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device with this orientation */,
  "pitch" REAL /* Pitch of the device */,
  "roll" REAL /* Roll of the device */,
  "yaw" REAL /* Yaw of the device */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the orientation data */,
  "heading" REAL /* Heading of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("orientation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_orientations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_velocities" (
  "velocity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each velocity */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device with this velocity */,
  "x_velocity" REAL /* X-velocity of the device */,
  "y_velocity" REAL /* Y-velocity of the device */,
  "z_velocity" REAL /* Z-velocity of the device */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the velocity data */,
  "speed" REAL /* Speed of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("velocity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_velocities_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_accelerations" (
  "acceleration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each acceleration */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device with this acceleration */,
  "x_acceleration" REAL /* X-acceleration of the device */,
  "y_acceleration" REAL /* Y-acceleration of the device */,
  "z_acceleration" REAL /* Z-acceleration of the device */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the acceleration data */,
  "jerk" REAL /* Jerk of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("acceleration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_accelerations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_data" (
  "environmental_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environmental data */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity as a percentage */,
  "atmospheric_pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in pascals */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the environmental data */,
  "light_level" INTEGER /* Light level in the environment */,
  "noise_level" INTEGER /* Noise level in the environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("environmental_data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "num_attempted_measurements" (
  "num_attempted_measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each number of attempted measurements */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement */,
  "num_attempted_measurements" INTEGER /* Number of attempted measurements */,
  "attempted_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("num_attempted_measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_num_attempted_measurements_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "num_successful_measurements" (
  "num_successful_measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each number of successful measurements */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement */,
  "num_successful_measurements" INTEGER /* Number of successful measurements */,
  "successful_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the success */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("num_successful_measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_num_successful_measurements_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distance_std_dev_m" (
  "distance_std_dev_m_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distance standard deviation in meters */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement */,
  "distance_std_dev_m" REAL /* Distance standard deviation in meters */,
  "distance_reliability" REAL /* Reliability score of the distance measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_std_dev_m_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_std_dev_m_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ssids" (
  "ssid_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SSID */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement */,
  "ssid" TEXT /* SSID */,
  "security_protocol" TEXT /* Security protocol used by the SSID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ssid_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ssids_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "buildings" (
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each building */,
  "building_name" TEXT /* Name of the building */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the building */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the building */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the building */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "floors" (
  "floor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each floor */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* ID of the building */,
  "floor_number" INTEGER /* Number of the floor */,
  "floor_name" TEXT /* Name of the floor */,
  "floor_area" REAL /* Area of the floor in square meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("floor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_floors_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)
